text.text:
Lovely hotel in Hoi An! This is my second time staying in Almanity (1st: June 2023 and now: January 2026) and love it even more than the first time! Only a short walk to the river/main strip. The resort also offers bikes as an alternative to walking. Breakfast is also included, offering a range of Asian and western options.

Staff were incredibly friendly and hospitable, and were easily able to arrange a spa package, tickets to events and tourist attractions, private driver to Ba Na Hills, and airport transfer.

A massive thank you to Lien for her hospitality, who greeted us with a smile every day and made sure we had what we needed to make our stay the most enjoyable it could be. And thank you to Ba for driving us to and from Ba Na Hills, waiting for us, and ensuring we had a very smooth ride.

Couldn’t recommend this hotel and resort more to anyone staying in Hoi An.

text.text:
Our second visit to this resort and it’s is still amazing. Staff are friendly and so helpful. The restaurants are great with the breakfast buffet being one of the best I’ve ever seen! Room service menu is great and food is so yummy.
Very conveniently located just a 5 minute walk to Hoi An old town. So many food and shops located close by.
The resorts’ wellness spa was amazing - we had a 1 hour couples massage with a 1/2 hour jacuzzi and felt like jelly mush afterwards! They were so great!
The only downside is that we booked an accessible room which as I’m semi-ambulant is fine, very roomy and suits my needs perfectly. However if you were a full time wheelchair user you would have difficulty manoeuvring around the bathroom, onto the bed and wouldn’t have access to the pool or wellness spa which all have stepped access.
I’ve put captions about the accessibility of the room on the photos. The foyer, restaurants and walkways are all accessible with ramps where there are steps. Just not into the pool or day spa areas.

text.text: We visited this hotel in October and it is a great hotel.  Conveniently located about a 10 minute walk from the old town or very close if you use a Grab taxi.  What I liked about this hotel it is quiet, conveniently located to shops and conveniences and restaurants without being too near the old town.  Which I liked as the old own can be very busy (lovely but busy).  We had a loft room which was fun, but not if you have mobility issues or small children as the stairs to the loft room may be a little steep in the night, but don't let that put you off as there as a lot of room options to choose from.  The hotel grounds and pool are just amazing, clean and the staff are incredibly helpful, but not overpowering.  They generally want to make your stay perfect.  The breakfast is vast and plentiful with a LOT of options.  Everything restocked and areas impeccably clean.  Staff very helpful at breakfast, explaining options and what hot options were available etc.  The main restaurant is lovely as well and we visited twice for lunch, plenty of options and beautifully presented.  Please note that there are other restaurants and food options nearby in the surrounding area but my husbands back was not good on the days we stayed so visiting the onsite restaurant suited us and it didn't disappoint.  Overall a fab hotel, which I would definitely stay at again.

text.text:
We had a wonderful 2 night stay at Little Riverside Hoi An and wish we were able to stay longer. From the moment we arrived, the service from the reception staff and doorman was attentive, friendly and helpful.

We arrived 1-2hrs early from our check-in time and were treated to iced drinks, hand towel and small chocolate cake which we appreciated after a long train and taxi ride from Da Nang. Staff recommended us nearby spots to visit and were always asking how everything was 😁

We relaxed at the spa/massage downstairs while waiting for our room to be ready which was also wonderful. We were assigned a city view room on lv5 with the outdoor pool which we enjoyed.

Complimentary breakfast selection was also incredible with egg station, vietnamese soup of the day, great selections of local delicacies such as banh beo as well as western options too! Cafe sua đa is also available upon request (highly recommend very tasty 😁)

One of the best hotels and experiences we have ever stayed at and will definitely be returning to this hotel if we ever return to Hoi An in the future!

text.text:
My Mum and I had booked to stay at this hotel for six nights to celebrate her 70th birthday. Sadly, three days into our stay (on my Mum’s actual birthday), we received the devastating news that my Dad (my Mum’s husband) had suddenly and unexpectedly passed away back in Australia.
The entire team at Little Riverside showed us incredible kindness and compassion during that awful morning, even arranging a complimentary transfer to the airport, which meant more to us than we can put into words. Special thanks to Amber that morning and throughout our stay, along with Queenie.
In the short time we were in Vietnam, the hospitality was exceptional. The river-view rooms are beautiful, the pool is stunning, and the location is perfect. Thoughtful touches like deep baths, a free shuttle to the beach, and bicycles for guests really set this hotel apart.
Although we didn’t get to experience everything due to our circumstances, we truly hope to return to complete what we started.
With gratitude, Nicole and Christine

text.text:
This is perfectly located cracker of a boutique hotel in Hoi A right on the riverside as the name suggests. As soon as we walked in on arrival, we were warmly and affectionately welcomed by the adorable Aimee who looked after us throughout our stay to the highest possible standards. She is an incredible asset to the hotel and had just the best attitude, energy and smile. All the team are brilliant to be fair but Aimee was just amazing 🥰

The Little Riverside is in a cracking location to walk in to all the hustle and bustle of tailors, restaurants, bars, river boats, bridges, taxis etc and then get back to the sanctuary of the hotel and fall into a really large, comfy bed and spotlessly clean room. A huge bath, ace shower, fluffy towels - The housekeeping team do a superb job!! 👏🏻

Breakfast is comprehensive with from breads, croissants, jams, meats, it also does bacon, tomatoes, has a chef station for any type of egg and omelettes plus several hot and cold Vietnamese dishes, sushi and amazing coffees.

The spa was outstanding and in my 6 nights there I had 5 brilliant massages and facials - absolute bliss. Cherry (pictured) and all the other spa therapists were so good at their jobs.

Jenny the manager did a great job organising all the events for Christmas and it was so well done. The atmosphere was so contagious and fun.

If I’m ever back in Hoi An I will stay back here - even just to see Aimee and the team again. Well done everyone! 👏🏻👏🏻👏🏻

text.text: There are only a few cities around the world that I would want to revisit again and again, and Hoi An is one of them. Packed in a small area of ancient town are ancient temples, shops and restaurants, historical sites, unique structures, a river with lantern boats, night markets, bars and clubs, hotels and hostels and tens of thousands of tourists per day.  The people of Hoi An are extremely friendly.  You will find a good photo spot at almost every corner or alley ways.  This is what makes this town unique.  You can take picture until you run out of storage space and still there is more to discover.  At night is when all the people come out and all the action happen. There are lots to do and lots to see. Make sure to have seafood at the end corner of the night market, it is cheap and delicious.

text.text:
If there is one place in Vietnam that I would tell everyone not to miss, it is Hoi An.

During the day, the Old Town feels peaceful and timeless. The yellow colonial buildings, narrow lanes, and historic houses create a charming atmosphere that reflects its past as a bustling trading port. Walking around on foot is a pleasure — every corner feels photogenic and full of character.

But Hoi An becomes truly magical at night.

As the sun sets, thousands of colorful lanterns light up the streets and the Thu Bon River. The reflections on the water create a dreamy, romantic scene that is unforgettable. Taking a small boat ride and releasing a floating lantern on the river is a simple yet meaningful experience — calm, beautiful, and symbolic.

The people here are warm and welcoming, which makes the whole experience even more special. Shop owners and locals are friendly, patient, and happy to share their culture.

Another highlight is the tailoring. Hoi An is famous for its custom-made clothing. You can have a well-fitted suit or elegant dress tailored within just a few hours to a day, at very reasonable prices considering the craftsmanship and quality. It’s a rare combination of speed, skill, and affordability.

Hoi An is more than a destination — it’s an atmosphere. A place where history, culture, beauty, and hospitality come together effortlessly. A true must-visit when traveling to Vietnam.

text.text:
Hoi An is one of the most beautiful and atmospheric places in Vietnam. The ancient town, with its glowing lanterns, yellow walls, and riverside charm, feels like stepping into another world. Whether you visit during the day or at night, every corner has something picturesque and full of character.

The food is exceptional, the people are warm, and the mix of Vietnamese, Chinese, and Japanese heritage creates a unique cultural experience. Walking through the old streets, trying local dishes, taking a boat ride on the river — everything feels peaceful and unforgettable.

Yes, it can get busy during peak hours, but the beauty and atmosphere more than make up for it. If you’re traveling in Vietnam, Hoi An is an absolute must-visit — a place that stays with you long after you leave.

text.text:
Hoi An Ancient Town is well worth visiting if you can get past the heavy concentration of shops and focus on the history and atmosphere.

Take time to visit the old merchant houses and temples, which give real context to how the town developed.

In the early evening, the lanterns light up the streets and river, creating a beautiful setting. A short boat ride on the river and releasing a lantern adds to the experience.

There are plenty of cafés to stop for coffee, and the town is also popular for custom-made clothing if you have time. Ducking into narrow alleys often leads to quieter restaurants away from the main flow.

The area is very walkable and best enjoyed in the early evening, though you do need to stay alert for bikes and motorbikes.

Simply walking through the streets, sounds, and lights is an experience in itself.

text.text:
I visited Bánh Mì Phượng in Hoi An and it definitely lived up to its reputation. This place is famous for a reason. The shop was busy, but the staff worked efficiently and the line moved quickly.

The bánh mì was absolutely delicious. The bread was crispy on the outside and soft on the inside, creating the perfect texture. The fillings were generous and full of flavor, with a great balance of savory meat, fresh vegetables, herbs, and sauce. Every bite was packed with taste, and the ingredients felt fresh and well prepared.

text.text:
Let’s be real: Bánh Mì Phượng is probably the most famous sandwich shop on the planet thanks to Anthony Bourdain. Because of that, it’s now a mandatory stop for every tourist. But after standing in an insanely long line for what felt like forever, I have to say it: it is totally not worth the hype.🤔🤨
​The scene outside is pure madness—crowded, sweaty, and stressful. And the sandwich? Honestly, it’s just okay. It’s not bad, but it’s definitely nothing special. With the massive volume they pump out daily, the soul of the bread feels lost. It’s a mass-produced experience where you’re just another number in a queue. If you’re looking for a "culinary miracle," you’re going to be disappointed. It’s the kind of place you visit just to say you’ve been there, not because the food actually blows your mind.
​The only upside is the location in the heart of the ancient town, surrounded by those iconic yellow walls and pretty flowers. It’s a nice view, but it doesn't make the mediocre bread taste any better. If you want to taste real, authentic Vietnamese Bánh Mì, Hoi An has so many other hidden gems like Bánh Mì Madam Khánh or small street carts that offer way more flavor with zero wait time. Skip the tourist trap, save your time, and go explore the local spots instead. Your taste buds (and your patience) will thank you.🇻🇳

text.text:
One of the best co-working spots we've ever had the fortune of working from, my partner and I loved it so much we're planning to return in a few months after our honeymoon!

Especially loved the ice baths 3 times a week. Sitting in an ice bath, under the hot sun, looking out over the rice fields was such a delight!

Beautiful views over rice paddies and distant mountains, (much better than is possible to capture in photos), stunning sunsets.

Warm, friendly vibe with tonnes of events going on, though we were too busy with work to make the most of the social aspect. Always someone around to chat to in the lounge, yet dedicated quiet and AC spaces where it's easy to focus and work 24/7 without interruption. Lots of people around working, even really late at night.

Loads of thoughtful touches, free coffee, free big fluffy towels for showers and ice baths, foot cushions under many desks, a variety of good chairs (including plenty without arm rests - a win for those of us who have POTS/ can only sit cross-legged!)... it really feels like everything has been thought of and a lot of care has been taken to make it a great experience for members.

We can't wait to come back!

text.text:
Hoi An Roastery Coffee House, right in the midst of the old Town is a chill place to be at, especially on a Sunday afternoon.

We had 2 Coconut coffees, an Ameicano, a cappuccino, an egg coffee and a delicious banana cake to go with.

The coffees were great. Egg coffee was fantastic, it had an intense flavor which was super aromatic. Banana cake was mediocre but okay for a afternoon snack.

Sitting on the upper floor,  we had a great time chilling with a cool afternoon breeze blowing.

Ambience was one of a retro look. Floors were here was purely made of wood planks giving that rustic feel.

Overall, perfect for a pit stop and rest those tired legs while enjoying that cuppa.

Absolutely blew me away. I’ve had good coffee all over the world, but these guys are operating on another level.

I ordered the egg coffee first and… wow. It’s rich, silky, almost dessert-like without being heavy. Perfect balance of sweetness and that deep Vietnamese coffee punch. Easily the best egg coffee I’ve had in Vietnam.

Their pour-over was just as impressive clean, aromatic, and full of character. You can tell they’re roasting quality beans and actually care about the craft.

But the real standout was the service from Thanh Hà. Genuine warmth, great recommendations, and the kind of hospitality that makes you feel like you’ve been coming here for years.

If you’re in Hoi An and love coffee, this place is non-negotiable. Go for the egg coffee, stay for the pour-over, and enjoy some of the best service in town.

ChIJyUQ4aw72QTERCXl5YV-4U1w.editorial_summary: Theme park with adrenaline rides, a cable car & panoramic mountain views from its Golden Bridge.

text.text:
Here’s a clean, engaging, and positive rewrite of your review with a travel-friendly tone:

Ba Na Hills – Sun World is an amazing place and a must-visit attraction. We visited through a tour package booked at our hotel, which made the trip very convenient. Couples will especially enjoy this place — and as a bonus, you get one free beer at the entrance 🙃.

The atmosphere truly feels like a French colonial town, with plenty of fun activities to enjoy. There’s great food, beer, and entertainment, along with the long and scenic cable car ride, which is an experience on its own. The Golden Bridge is a dream spot for most visitors — perfect for unforgettable photos.

There are also many amusement rides, including exciting ones like Rock ’n’ Roll, making it fun for all age groups.

Tip: Visit as early as possible to fully explore the area and avoid crowds. Also, carry a light jacket, as it can get cool at the top.

Overall, it’s a fantastic experience with beautiful views, fun rides, and a unique European vibe — highly recommended!

text.text:
If I could give this place 100 stars I would. It was easily one of the coolest experiences of my 45 years of life. It is hard to put into words what this place is truly like , but it is magical and they are still building more!

First off it’s huge and takes a full day of walking and cable cars to see everything so keep that in mind when bringing children. It is also about 10 degrees cooler than down below so dress accordingly. They do accept Visa so if you don’t bring cash you will be fine. They also sell rain coats and sweaters just in case. The food there is definitely better than we expected and so was the service. You can get a Grab to bring you back from there so no need to hire a driver for the day, but if you decide to do that we paid out driver 650,000 which is about $25 usd and he waited for us and we paid him at the very end.

It truly is something everyone should experience once in their life.

It won’t let me post pics or videos on this for some reason, but you will mostly likely take a Ton of them. So photographers bring your set ups because it’s amazing!

Lastly GET THE WOW PASS. We got the silver and it was worth every penny.

It won’t let me post pics or videos on this for some reason, but you will mostly likely take a Ton of them. So photographers bring your set ups because it’s amazing!

Lastly GET THE WOW PASS. We got the silver and it was worth every penny.

text.text: Ba Na Hills Sun World is an impressive and visually striking attraction, offering a unique experience unlike anywhere else in Vietnam. The cable car ride alone is worth the visit, with breathtaking panoramic views over lush mountains and valleys. The Golden Bridge and surrounding French-themed architecture are iconic, especially when shrouded in mist, which adds a rather magical and atmospheric charm. The landscaped gardens are beautifully maintained and provide plenty of photo opportunities. That said, the area can become extremely crowded, particularly around the main landmarks, which slightly detracts from the overall experience. Nevertheless, it remains a must-visit destination for its scenery, scale, and sheer spectacle.

For lunch, we ate at their buffet restaurant—massive variety of food options!  Very spacious too, plenty of seating.  Their new brewery, Sun Kraft Beer is pretty huge too, that’s where you can use your voucher for the free beer.

Overall, highly recommend visiting here!  We spent only 6 hours here and didn’t even come close to seeing everything.

text.text:
The area above the Golden Bridge in Da Nang is beautifully managed and offers a truly impressive experience. The ropeway that takes you up the steep hills is well-organized, and if you take the last ride of the day, you’ll be treated to a stunning waterfall view along the way. The waterfall is absolutely gorgeous and offers a clear, breathtaking sight from the ropeway.
While there’s quite a bit of construction due to the challenging terrain, the end result is impressive. There are various villages to explore, wonderful restaurants, and a host of adventure activities, including different types of swings and rides. The gardens are also beautiful, making it a memorable and enriching experience overall.

ChIJ2y5L4dQNQjERxeSLc68fbrw.editorial_summary: Network of 5 marble mountains with multiple caves, elaborate temples & viewing outlooks.

text.text:
A spiritual and cultural highlight in Da Nang – Highly recommended!
​After some rather "touristy" experiences in the city, the Marble Mountains were a breath of fresh air. This place offers everything I was looking for: deep culture, impressive history, and breathtaking views.
​The blend of natural limestone caves and beautifully integrated Buddhist pagodas creates a unique, spiritual atmosphere. Climbing the stairs is worth it for the panoramic views over the coast, but the highlight for me was the intricate stone carvings and the quiet energy inside the caves. It feels authentic and respectful of its heritage.
​If you appreciate history and want to experience the "real" Vietnam away from the flashy tourist traps, this is a must-see. It’s affordable, well-maintained, and truly memorable. 5 stars without hesitation!

text.text:
The Marble Mountains are a must-see when you are in Da Nang. It is a group of five hills with beautiful caves, old pagodas, and great viewpoints. There is so much to explore, and every cave feels like a hidden surprise.
You can take the elevator to go up for an extra charge, but the real fun is taking the stairs because you can see more of the beauty. It is not actually a long walk, and you can easily reach the top in about 20 minutes.
The caves inside are huge with amazing statues. This is one of the best places for photography and beautiful scenes. I really enjoyed climbing to the top to see the view of the beach and the city. Definitely worth a visit

text.text:
Han Market is a real local gem. Clean, friendly, and easy to explore -  not huge, but packed with authentic goods and very low prices.

The second floor has local tailors who can custom-fit clothes in about an hour, which is impressive. Sellers were welcoming and helpful, and the whole place felt well-run and tidy.

Locals did advise keeping your wallet and cash secure (standard market advice), but overall it felt safe and relaxed.

Highly recommended if you want a genuine local experience without the chaos of larger markets.

text.text:
Organized, just very crowded. Not recommended to buy pasalubong here to bring home. You can haggle, but the marked down price is still more expensive than what you would find in Lotte Mart. They accept cash, credit card, and some foreign currency as payment.

Great place to get clothes and souvenir. Got two plus sized girlie dresses for 400k vnd, and two plus sized linen pants for 350k vnd after some haggling. Plus size girlie clothes typically range 1.5x to 2x more expensive than that where I'm from.

Ref magnets range from 100 to 150 vnd for a pack of six. Some more intricate designs go for 40 to 50k vnd. Again, haggle especially if you are buying a lot.

Good, affordable local fare sold on the right side of the ground floor, exiting to Han River. They may not speak English, but I can feel the hospitality and desire to show how the beauty of Vietnam. Our vendor saw how surprised we were by the size of their avocado and gave us a free taste.

text.text:
The air con in the room was not fully serviceable, we could not operate it at our desired setting (it was too cold) and we could not turn it on (once we left the room and returned). They came twice to fix it but unsuccessful (5 night stay), thankfully the weather was a cool 21 Celsius so on the final 2 nights we had to open the windows for the night. Overall, the building is well maintained, the infinite pool on the roof is a must go. Gym equipment is decent.
Buffet breakfast was great, good selection (both western and local delights) and sufficient for vegetarians. However a good coffee machine would be nice, they served regular brewed coffee only. Like most places (in Vietnam), service staff MUST be trained to be assertive and polite when needed, example hotel guest using their bare hands to the food (breakfast buffet), instead of the tongs/serving gears.
Generally the hotel staff were friendly and accommodating. One unusual event happened when we needed some cutlery for our food we ordered in. To make it convenient and quick, I when to the 2 level restaurant to borrow some cutlery and had to wait 4 mins for the staff to generate an invoice for me to "sign" for the borrowed cutlery. Was told to return them upon check out, which we did. During checkout we were told we could just leave them in the room. Could not understand the need to sign for the cutlery and given another set of instructions later.

text.text:
We stayed at the hotel (not the villas) for one night as part of our Danang resort-hopping adventures with our very little toddler. The room was a very good size with a nice layout. The only nitpick is that the toilet did not have a bidet seat, which one would expect from a luxury resort in Asia.

The pools are a decent size and, relative to the number of guests, big enough. The beach was very private, clean and with very few people, but similar to most resorts in the area. The resort was very well-maintained, the grass was cut and green, and there was no garbage.

The hotel itself is about a 5-minute walk to the pools, restaurants, and beach, but there are shuttle carts available if you prefer not to walk. Be warned that the walkway is cobblestoned, so it’s not the easiest to walk on, but it adds to the place's aesthetics. The kids’ play area was a decent size, and my toddler liked it.

Breakfast was very well done; the made-to-order coffee and some cooked-to-order dishes, instead of everything being self-serve, were a nice touch. For dinner, there were only two restaurant options: a steakhouse-style restaurant by the beach and a Vietnamese restaurant. We had the hot pot. All dishes are à la carte. Prices are expensive compared to local restaurants but reasonable for foreigners, expect to spend about 500K–1M VND per person for dinner.

The staff (Hong) were great and very attentive. Overall, we had fun and would come again.

This resort ticks all the boxes for a stay that is not only relaxing but has a complete array of aesthetic restaurants and cafes as well as swimming pools. Lisa is an absolute star and if you are lucky enough to meet her; I assure you that your stay here will be absolutely perfect.

text.text:
Throughout my stay, I was deeply impressed by the kindness and professionalism of your staff.
The room condition and breakfast were both outstanding and exceeded my expectations.

The daily complimentary massage was truly a highlight of my stay—it was simply blissful each and every time.
Although there were some minor challenges with making reservations, the overall experience was so positive that it hardly mattered.

I would especially like to share one memorable moment.
On my last day, I asked a female staff member standing across from the check-in information desk if it was possible to exchange 500,000 VND.
At most hotels, I would have been told that it was not possible and advised to go to another desk.
However, your staff member kindly took the money herself, went directly to the information desk, requested the exchange on my behalf, and personally returned the exchanged money to me.

This small yet incredibly thoughtful gesture truly touched me.
My wife, who was standing next to me, also mentioned how deeply moved she was by such genuine attention to detail.

If I ever return to Da Nang, I will without hesitation choose Fusion Resort & Villas Da Nang again,
and I will wholeheartedly recommend it to all of my friends and acquaintances.

Thank you so much for helping us create such wonderful memories during our stay as we celebrated our 20th wedding anniversary.
It was truly an unforgettable experience.

ChIJLaS0-joYQjERNTjoKJ6-LdU.editorial_summary: Warm rooms in a modern river-view hotel with an outdoor pool, plus stylish dining & 2 bars.

text.text:
Novotel Danang Premier Han River is a very convenient city hotel—about 15 minutes by car from the airport and around a 15-minute walk to Han Market. One of the biggest advantages is the excellent Han River access and views: you can simply cross the road and be right by the river.

The staff were consistently friendly, and the room condition was good overall, so I would recommend this hotel to first-time visitors to Danang or anyone who wants to explore the city on foot.

As part of the Platinum member benefits, I was upgraded to a room facing toward the Thuan Phuoc Bridge area, with a wide open view of Danang Bay and the Son Tra Peninsula. The panoramic scenery—river, sea, and mountains—was even better than expected and made it easy to relax even while staying in the hotel. The gym was also spacious and well equipped, and working out in the morning while looking over the Han River was a great start to the day. Housekeeping and cleanliness were also very satisfactory.

The only area that could be improved is the swimming pool. If the hotel could adjust the number and placement of sunbeds and add more greenery around the pool, it would feel even more comfortable for resting while enjoying the view.

The Premier Lounge evening cocktails were excellent, and the Han River night view was a perfect way to end the day. The lounge staff always greeted guests with warm smiles and kind service—among the best I have experienced compared to other hotels. I was also touched to receive a birthday cake during my stay, even though I had not mentioned my birthday in advance. My sincere thanks to the lounge and reception teams.

Overall, I truly appreciate all the staff for making my trip more memorable, and I hope to return again on my next visit to Danang.

text.text:
Over the years, we have stayed at many Novotel properties, but our experience at Novotel Danang Premier Han River clearly stands out. In fact, it is the best Novotel we have stayed in so far. What made the difference was not a single feature, but the consistency of warmth, professionalism, and genuine care we felt throughout the hotel.

A special highlight for us was the Premier Lounge. Ms. Rosa, Ms. Kile, Ms. Nami, Ms. Luccie, Ms. Nga, Mr. Tino, and Mr. The welcomed us with kindness and attentiveness that made every visit feel relaxed and personal. Their calm professionalism and friendly presence created an atmosphere we truly enjoyed coming back to.

Behind the scenes, we would also like to thank Ms. Dieu and Ms. Tam from the kitchen. Their dedication could be felt in the small details that made the lounge experience run so smoothly, adding quiet comfort to our daily routine.

Breakfast each morning was shaped by the warm service of Mr. Trung, Ms. Vy, and Ms. Huong. Their friendliness and attentive manner helped set a pleasant tone for the day and made breakfast feel unhurried and genuinely enjoyable.

And to all the other wonderful team members we encountered along the way: even if we cannot list every name, your kindness, your smiles and your constant willingness to help left a deep impression on us. You made the hotel feel alive in the best possible way.

To the entire Novotel Danang Premier Han River team, thank you for a stay filled with warmth, care, and outstanding hospitality. We look forward to returning.

text.text:
I’m extremely disappointed with this coworking space.
For three days I asked the staff to handle a situation where one person was constantly coughing in the shared room. They admitted they have health rules — but completely ignored them.

Now almost everyone on the floor is sick, including me. When I came today to pick up my monitor, another person next to my old desk also said she just got sick.

The girl who caused all this even told the staff she wanted to move because she “got sick from the air conditioner.” But people don’t get sick from air conditioning — they get sick from viruses. And her cough was deep, loud, and disgusting — she was coughing for more than a week straight.

Even after my complaint, she kept coming to the shared space every day. That’s extremely disrespectful and unhygienic, especially for a grown woman.

This was 100% preventable. If they had simply moved her to another room, nobody would have gotten sick. Instead, they did nothing, and now the whole space is infected.

Their so-called “health policy” is just empty words. Absolutely irresponsible management. Avoid this place if you care about your health and professionalism.

text.text:
It’s one of the more popular coworking spaces in Da Nang so keep in mind at certain days and hours it can be jammed packed and it’ll be harder to find a good comfortable seat. There’s 8 floors and on the 1st floor is the lobby and a cafe where if you sign up for a coworking option you’ll get a discount on your purchase.

They’ve got multiple service options such as a hot desk which means every time you arrive you can pick a seat but it’s not a fixed reserved spot for you. There’s a fixed desk, private office (5-25 people) an event space (15-100 people), a co-living space, and even a virtual office. Each one varies for if you want hourly, daily, weekly, monthly and how many people but they sure got a lot of options.

text.text:
After working in the co-working space for two months, I decided to move into the hotel as well and it turned out to be the perfect decision. This is truly a great place to work, live, and meet new people. The room is very nice, the bed is comfortable, and the prices are surprisingly affordable.

What really makes this place special, though, are the people who work here. Everyone is incredibly kind, welcoming, and approachable. Snow, the manager, along with the housekeeping and reception teams, create such a warm and friendly atmosphere. You can talk to them as if they were friends. They are young, speak English well, and always have a great sense of humor.

Today is December 24th (Christmas), and yesterday the hotel organized a Christmas party. We all received lovely little gifts, enjoyed free food and drinks, and had an amazing karaoke night with songs in many different languages. It was such a fun and thoughtful experience.

I highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a comfortable stay with an especially friendly and caring team.

Unfortunately, I had to abandon my 4 out of 7 days in the package that I bought because of the pungent food smell. It really is a pity.

I have been staying and working at ACE Coworking for over 4 months and it has been an amazing experience. The atmosphere is both focused and welcoming, making it easy to stay productive while still connecting with others.

Every day there are networking opportunities with fresh fruits and cakes which creates a natural way to meet people and share ideas.

What really makes ACE stand out is the community vibe. The members are mostly digital nomads, entrepreneurs, and startup founders so conversations are inspiring and motivating. There are also plenty of activities outside the office such as beach hangouts and pickleball which are a perfect balance to the workday.

Overall ACE Coworking strikes a rare balance. It offers a good focus atmosphere for deep work and at the same time a strong community with daily networking and fun activities. Highly recommended for anyone looking to work and connect in Da Nang.

text.text:
🏠 My Home Away from Home in Da Nang! Best Vibe Ever! 💖✨

I came to Da Nang with a Japanese nomad team, but then decided to stay solo. Honestly, I was a bit anxious alone... until I found Hana's Coworking. Staying here was the BEST decision of my trip! 😭🙌

✨ Why I fell in love with this place:

Hana-san is an Angel! 🥺💖 She is so kind and welcomes you like family. I felt instantly at home. I was so moved that she kindly agreed to an interview when I told her my family runs a coworking space in Japan! 🎙️🇯🇵 Her passion for community inspired me so much. Thank you, Hana-san!! 🔥

Made Friends despite my English! 🤝🌍 Everyone is so friendly here! One member saw me taking notes and taught me about the "Granola" app 📱💡. We became friends naturally. The vibe is just magical! ✨

Perfect Oasis 🌿💻 It's super chill and green. The Wi-Fi is super fast (perfect for Zoom!), and the garden is the best place to relax! 😋

If you are looking for a place with Real Heart & Community, this is it. I will definitely come back home here! Love you, Hana's Coworking! 🥰👋

The Coworking that feels like home, literally!

The Coworking is nested on the 3rd floor of Hana's house, and she's actively preparing to extend it on the second floor as well.

The space itself is very cozy, with a very nice view, especially at sunset time. AC is brand new and will keep you cool the whole time.

There's also a relax area outside of the main space, as well a rooftop area where you can hangout outside the main sunny hours.

You get free monitors, as well as free tea and coffee. It's the cheapest price on the beach side.

It's in a local area so it's nice if you want to be away from the main touristy areas in My An/An Thuong/Son Tra.

Hana herself is a gem, and will help you with whatever you need. She's built a nice community over the last year, which is why people love her so much.

So if you want to experience the more local side of Da Nang with a welcoming community a little feel like home feeling, Hana's Coworking Space is perfect for you!

text.text:
I visited Bánh Xèo Bà Dưỡng on a sunny afternoon and it turned out to be one of the best bánh xèo experiences in Da Nang. The place has a relaxed, local feel with simple tables set up under a shaded area that keeps it breezy and comfortable even in the midday heat. Locals come here regularly, and you can tell from the steady stream of regular customers that this is the real deal rather than a tourist trap.

The bánh xèo itself is the highlight. Each pancake comes out piping hot and crispy around the edges with that perfect golden color you hope for. The batter is light yet sturdy enough to hold the generous filling of juicy pork, fresh shrimp, and crunchy bean sprouts. The combination of textures between the crisp outside and tender inside makes every bite satisfying. What really sets it apart here is the freshness of the ingredients and the balance of flavors. The pancake isn’t greasy, and the fillings taste bright and well seasoned rather than heavy.

The lettuce and herb platter that comes with the bánh xèo is generous and fresh. Wrapping bites in leafy greens and dipping them in the tangy, slightly sweet fish sauce elevates the whole dish. The sauces are well balanced, with just enough acidity and umami to lift each bite.

Service is quick and friendly. Even when the place gets busy, orders come out fast and the team keeps everything moving smoothly. There’s no fuss, just good food that makes you want to dig in.

If you are in Da Nang and want an authentic, delicious bánh xèo that locals swear by, Bánh Xèo Bà Dưỡng is worth seeking out. The crisp pancakes, fresh herbs, and lively atmosphere make it a great meal for lunch or a casual dinner.

I would say if you are in Da Nang for the first time give this place a try. It gives me din tai Fung vibes. Food is delicious but not exceptional but what stands out is the view of the Han River and the decor of the restaurant.

The staffs were nice and attentive even though it was packed. I only had to wait 5 minute for our table without reservation on a Thursday lunch.

I would order the special grilled fish, nem ran (shrimp egg roll stuff with meat) scorched rice, ca kho tho (grouper) and morning glory again.

I would’ve given this place a 5 star but the grilled squid came out cold and their barley was any fish in my ca kho. You can skip the banh beo here and banh it ram.

Definitely go to this place once for the experience but if you wanna eat good food for cheap then ask the local. This restaurant menu is too big so they are master of none.

text.text:
Madame Lan is a beautifully designed restaurant with an impressive setting and layout. The décor and interior are well done, and the open, airy concept makes the space feel fresh and relaxing. We really appreciated having the option to sit either indoors or outdoors, both offering a lovely view of the Han River.
The food came out quickly, which is a plus, though it would have been even better if it were served a bit hotter. The menu selection is excellent, with a wide variety of well-prepared dishes to choose from.
That said, there are a few areas that could be improved. Our table had not been wiped before we were seated, and paper napkins were empty—small details, but noticeable for a restaurant of this level. These preparations should ideally be handled prior to seating guests.
Pricing is higher compared to many local restaurants, but that’s expected given Madame Lan’s Michelin Guide recognition and strong reputation. Comparing it directly to smaller or more casual eateries isn’t really fair.
Overall, we had a good experience and enjoyed the food and atmosphere. With a bit more attention to table readiness and food temperature, this place could easily reach the next level.

text.text:
A long-standing establishment with an extensive menu featuring central Vietnamese specialties such as mì quảng, nem lụi, grilled seafood and tender beef over vegetables.

Bánh Căn Nhân Tôm Dừa – 106,000 VND
Small round rice cakes baked in a special mould with small cavities, almost like miniature waffles.

Filled with freshly ground shrimp and served with a rich coconut sauce and fresh herbs. The flavor leaned noticeably sweet, perhaps slightly too sweet, alongside the savory notes. Crispy on the outside and soft inside. Overall, a well-executed and enjoyable dish.

Gỏi Cuốn Tôm Trứng Muối – 240,000 VND (5 rolls)
Fresh spring rolls with shrimp, salted egg and herbs, a variation on the classic dish, served with peanut sauce.

The ingredients were very fresh and the presentation was refined. However, the salted egg was intensely salty and dominated both the peanut sauce and the overall balance of the roll.

Nước Ép Ổi – 85,000 VND
The portion was very small relative to the price. The flavor was flat and uninspiring, suggesting the fruit was not at an optimal stage of ripeness for juicing. Disappointing, especially at that price point.

Summary:
There is clearly a professional kitchen behind the operation. However, in the dishes we tried, the precision seemed to focus more on presentation and plating than on culinary balance and depth of flavor.

text.text:
The food is just OK, and the prices are high compared to the general market.
If you’re looking to sample Vietnamese dishes from all three regions, this restaurant meets the criteria of being quick and offering a wide variety. It’s suitable for tourists who don’t have much time and just want a brief overview of Vietnamese cuisine.

However, it wouldn’t be accurate to say that all the dishes are delicious. Overall, the food is average, around 6–7/10.
If you’re looking for really good food, it’s better to find smaller places that focus on just 1–4 dishes.

Tip: Ask for drinks with less sugar when ordering.

Ps: A nice detail worth praising is that they provide small baskets for guests to place their belongings under the table.

text.text:
12/30/25. Danang's Michelin Recommended fish and crab paste/balls noodle soup.

Made an out of the way trip to this eatery for their Michelin dish. To those planning to try it out too... manage your expectation since this is a local no frills eatery.

Ordered the fish and crab paste/balls noodle. It was a heaping bowl of fish cakes with one piece of crab ball. The crab Fat was not visible nor palpable. The clear soup had mild sweet flavour owing to the pineapple in the broth.

Initially I found the broth to be too subtle in taste but with the addition of fish sauce, chilis and herbs, the bowl became flavorful.

They have to two Michelin recommendations for 2024 and 2025 but the food is still affordable. Still wish that more crab is visible and palpable.

text.text:
Amazing broth but only when you add the lime, picked onions & spicy minced garlic (and a bit of the fermented shrimp paste)!!!

Not a big onion/garlic person so ate majority of my bowl without it and thought it was just ok, like an oden / yong tau foo soup given that it’s slightly sweet with lots of fishcakes, but when I added it, that’s when the broth really became very tasty!

Fishcakes have a nice bite, not spongy & soft. Goes well with the chilli sauce! Not quite a fan of the bun rieu, so would recommend just the normal one.

I arrived quite late in the evening, and even though it was already past their closing time, the staff kindly waited for me. That alone left a really good impression.

The overall system reminded me a lot of MiuMiu Spa in Ho Chi Minh City — from the way the service is structured to how attentive the therapists are. therapist maintaining just the right pressure throughout the session. It felt seamless and very professional.

The massage quality was honestly excellent. The use of herbal compresses, hot stones, and essential oils was especially impressive. After the session, they served tea and light snacks, which was a lovely touch. The spa itself was clean and had a calm, relaxing atmosphere — ideal for unwinding after a long day.

Definitely a place I’d return to on my next trip to Da Nang.

text.text:
Wow, what a fantastic spot. I'm so glad I chose this one out of handful of spas I found from Google! It's really hard to tell with fake reviews everywhere nowadays which ones are real and legitimately good.

I booked via Whatsapp. They have so many ways to reach them to make an appt, but I think it's recommended and not required. Go 15 minutes prior do you can enjoy some candied ginger and coconut plus tea. You choose an oil if you picked aroma massage. The value for this experience is amazing for what you get.

You fill out a little questionnaire and can tell the receptionist any other preferences. I asked for strong pressure, and they delivered. I wasn't sure if they would because I had tried another place for a foot massage that was eh. This spot is amazing!

After the massage and when you change back into your clothes, you return the locker key, and they have you go to a room with mango, candied lotus, and tea. It's very aestheticly pleasing and relaxing. I fell asleep almost quite a few times despite the strong massage and was able to relax and melt some fatigue away from my trip. Thank you so much!!

text.text:
I am very disappointed with my visit to this salon. After so many glowing reviews, I truly expected an exceptional experience, but unfortunately, the result was the opposite.
The beginning was lovely — tea, a consultation form, choosing the oil, a beautiful changing area with thoughtful details, clean and pleasantly scented showers, and even a rose-petal foot ritual. The atmosphere was charming and promising.
However, I was surprised to be asked to pay immediately upon arrival, which felt unusual and slightly concerning. I chose an oil massage described beautifully in the menu and was expecting a high-quality, professional treatment.
Instead, the massage consisted mostly of simple up-and-down strokes without real technique. When I politely asked for more structured massage movements, the therapist left to bring a receptionist into the room while I was almost undressed, interrupting both my privacy and my session. I was then asked how exactly I wanted the massage to be done, which felt surprising for a salon with such a reputation. I was offered a different therapist and a different treatment, but I had specifically chosen this service and was limited in time, as I am a mother of a young child. I declined and asked to simply complete the session.
The pressure later became too strong, and when I asked to reduce it, another staff member entered again and offered to stop the massage and refund me. While I appreciate the refund, the overall handling of the situation felt unprofessional and lacking sensitivity. It would have been more appropriate to allow me to finish the treatment and then discuss my concerns afterward.
Thank you for the refund. However, for a young mother, time and relaxation are far more valuable — and unfortunately, those cannot be returned.

This is definitely a must go to massage shop in Danang!!! First, the space is very relaxing and spacious, giving you that luxury relaxing vibe. They first give you some tea and natural healthy snacks for you to start relaxing, then takes you upstairs to gently spa your feet with flower petals and scrubs. I got a 1hour foot massage, and its more than enough. While the massage, they do a complimentary cucumber facial so you can relax fully, even if its just a foot massage!!
After the massage they make sure you take your time to gather yourself and relax, as they give you tea with seasonal fruit, for me mango!! All of their food taste so good and healthy, it was clearly a detox! The place is super clean too, and the price is cheap. Would definitely recommend!
My recommend:
Lotus foot massage 60 mins
Masseuse: N.327 Mis.Ly

ChIJ7xtm7rMXQjER7ye2fxPfbFE.formatted_address: Lô 419, 422 Ngô Thì Sĩ, Bắc Mỹ An, Ngũ Hành Sơn, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

text.text:
We tried here milk based coffee and Gesha shot, also ordered caprese salad and grilled chicken sandwich. The salad was fresh, although it'd be nice to serve it with flaky sea salt and black pepper so the customer can choose between it and sauce that is served as an accompaniment.
I wouldn't say my husband and I are coffee gourmets, but the coffee was really good (more expensive than most local places, comparable to Starbucks probably, but much tastier - may Starbucks' fan forgive me 😼). We also didn't have to wait very long and had no issues with connecting to WiFi. We also enjoyed watching at koi fishes outside while waiting for our order.
A nice plus is the polite staff who speak excellent English.
Cons: it's easier to check the menu online while you're standing in line because there's only one and it's right there on the ordering counter, which isn't very convenient. And if you're not a coffee fan, you should be wary there are no other drinks on the menu, although it'd be great to add at least water or juices.

Everything felt well thought out, from the brewing to the presentation. Definitely a must-visit if you’re into specialty coffee or just want to try something different in a really nice setting.

text.text:
This XLIII Specialty Coffee, located in the center of Da Nang, is truly a hidden gem that I discovered during my first visit to the city.

The café offers a spacious, comfortable, and well-designed environment, making it an ideal place for afternoon coffee, casual meetings, or even focused work sessions.

What sets XLIII apart from typical cafés is its wide selection of coffee beans. U can choose from different bean varieties at varying price points, and the roasting style can be customized according to personal preference. This level of attention to detail makes the coffee experience quite unique. The average price is around VND 120,000 per cup, which is reasonable for specialty coffee of this quality.

In addition to coffee, the café also offers a variety of pastries and snacks to enjoy alongside your drink.

And according to the staff, XLIII has four other branches across Vietnam, including two in Ho Chi Minh City. I will definitely be visiting those locations in the future.

text.text: I had an incredible experience at this specialty coffee shop. The moment you walk in, you’re greeted by a warm, minimalist aesthetic soft lighting, and an inviting atmosphere that makes you want to stay awhile. What truly sets them apart is their deep commitment to ethically sourcing beans, partnering directly with farmers and cooperatives to ensure fair pay and sustainable growing practices. They’re transparent about their supply chain. On top of that, the staff is exceptionally professional and knowledgeable, always eager to share insights about origin profiles, processing methods, and brew techniques, making every visit both delicious and educational. Special thanks to master barista Yun for having me taste the rarest coffee in the world!

text.text:
XLIII Specialty Coffee in Da Nang is a true gem. The space is stylish, comfortable, and perfect for both relaxing and focused work. What really made our visit unforgettable was the lovely manager, Yun ☺️—a fluent English speaker who patiently and enthusiastically guided us through every step of our dream coffee experience, always with a smile.

We live in Chiang Mai, where quality coffee is a huge part of our lives, and Yun truly made our Vietnam trip extra special. We tried the Competition Coffee III from Colombia, and it was absolutely out of this world.

It’s also an excellent spot for remote workers, incredibly pet-friendly, and just has a great vibe overall. When we move to Da Nang, this will definitely be our base ☕🐾✨

text.text: Wow this place is amazing! You must try here! Down a little quiet street away from the busy road, keep going until you see the little blue circle sign. Very nice atmosphere, good calm music and peaceful setting. My daughter had the ice cube coffee, she said the best coffee she's ever had. I had the cold chocolate and it was great. For food we had the fruitbowl and chocolate croissant, both delicious 🤤😍💖

text.text: Tucked away in a little alley this place is such a cute little find. It was recommended to me from a friend and I’m so glad I came by. The salt coffee was unlike other coffees I tried before. Very unique and I enjoyed the space. Perfect for journaling and having a slow morning to take in Hoi An.

text.text:
During our stay in Hoi An, we had coffee at The Espresso Station twice. The place is very nicely decorated, the waiter is friendly, and both ordering and receiving the drinks are very fast. The restroom was clean, which is always appreciated.
My girlfriend only drinks vegan milk, which was available here. I tried the egg coffee once and the dripping coffee once. The egg coffee was a bit too sweet for my taste, but that’s just personal preference. Overall, a very pleasant café that we can recommend.

text.text: This cafe and coffee roaster evokes modern Hội An and is an excellent spot tucked away from the bustle of Old Town - despite being in the middle of it. Cozy, covered patio, clean. Excellent drink menu with modern elevated options, and some pastries, too. Try your classic Vietnamese drinks, such as salt coffee, coconut coffee, egg coffee, and so on - but with espresso. Real Vietnamese coffee, roasted on the spot. Kind service and great drink presentation. I'd love to see more places in Hội An like this - and that's what makes this cafe special.

ChIJH7cKY9YZQjERcKzAn0dYp7g.maps_query: Brilliant Top Bar, Da Nang, Vietnam
ChIJH7cKY9YZQjERcKzAn0dYp7g.formatted_address: 162 Bạch Đằng, Hải Châu, Đà Nẵng 550000, Vietnam

text.text:
Brilliant Top Bar

Amazing views from the rooftop. You can clearly see the Han River and Dragon Bridge, especially beautiful in the evening when the city lights turn on. One of the better spots in Da Nang if you want to sit above the city and just take it in.

Food was a very pleasant surprise. Good dish selection and everything we tried was super delicious, not just bar snacks. Presentation and taste were both on point.

Service was excellent. Staff were attentive, friendly, and well organized, even when the place got busy. You feel looked after without being rushed.

Overall, one of the best places in Da Nang for views, food, and service combined. Definitely worth a visit, especially in the evening.

text.text: This was an unexpected surprise. Walking in the atmosphere pulls you in. When you see the view you have to stop for a second and appreciate it. Sunset dinners here are a must. The food is excellent but the service is what you will find in Michelin  establishment. I highly recommend this place.

text.text: Brilliant Top Bar is located on the top floor of the Brilliant Hotel. It offers an open, expansive view with panoramic scenery of both sides of the Han River and the Dragon Bridge. The bar is beautifully decorated and has a great atmosphere.

text.text:
Extremely disappointing service and ignored reservation!
​I am beyond disappointed with my experience at Brilliant Top Bar. I had specifically requested a table with a direct view of the Dragon Bridge in my reservation. Despite arriving 5 minutes early, I was placed in the 3rd row with a mediocre view. When I complained, the staff completely ignored my request.
​The service timing was a disaster. I asked to order after the Dragon Show to enjoy the spectacle, but was told I had to order immediately, order comes in 30 Minutes. My appetizer arrived 5 minutes before the show started, followed by the oysters right as the fire show began. Instead of enjoying the moment, I was stressed between taking photos and food arriving at the wrong time.
​To make matters worse, the steak (part of a combo) was served lukewarm to cold. Before I even finished or asked for the bill, the waiter simply dropped the check for 850,000 VND on my table at 9:20 PM, basically telling me it was time to pay and leave. For these prices, I expected hospitality, not to be rushed out of the door after eating cold food. Save your money and go somewhere else where guests are actually valued.

text.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
We celebrated my birthday at Brilliant Top Bar, and everything was absolutely wonderful.

The service was outstanding — our waitress Hieu was incredibly kind, attentive, and made us feel truly welcome. She helped make this evening special.

The seafood was excellent. The oysters were amazing — very fresh and flavorful. My personal favorite was the arugula salad: the arugula was surprisingly tender and not bitter at all, which is rare. Definitely a top dish for me.

The steak was okay, but not something I would order again. However, the overall experience, atmosphere, and service were fantastic.

Thank you to the whole team for helping make my birthday celebration so memorable!
